should_trigger: Design a memory layer for our task orchestrator so it can retrieve prior agent runs and adapt the best previous plan to a new support workflow.
Our auto-orchestrator keeps repeating the same failed tool sequence. Build a case-based failure memory and retention policy so similar future tasks avoid it.
We have hundreds of completed data-cleaning workflows. How should the planner index, retrieve, revise, and retain cases when a new cleaning task arrives?
Create an orchestration strategy that reuses past incident-response traces, adapts them to new constraints, validates the adapted runbook, and stores lessons learned.
should_not_trigger: Summarize the history of case-based reasoning research for a literature review.
Write a SQL query to fetch the ten most recent rows from the cases table.
Design a rule-based router with no task history or persisted workflow examples.
edge_cases: We have only five prior workflow examples and no evaluator yet. Decide whether a case-based component is useful or whether we should first collect more cases and define validation.
The retrieved prior agent run is semantically similar but used a deprecated tool. Show how the orchestrator should adapt, validate, and decide whether to retain the new episode.
